q: Energy resources are all forms of fuel used in the modern world.
All energy sources have more or less of an impact on the environment.
Renewable energy sources are also known as conventional energy sources.
Renewable energy sources do not pollute the environment and natural surroundings.
Renewable energy sources require more expenditure.
Energy is a basic necessity for life.
Energy demand depends on the population.
Energy demand depends on the urbanization.
Energy demand depends on the industrialization.
Energy demand does not depend on net capital income.
Energy demand depends on the development of technologies.
Energy demand forecasting is crucial for ensuring future economic growth.
Energy demand forecasting is crucial for environmental security.
The industrial development is a reason for the increase in the global energy demand.
The population growth is a reason for the increase in global energy demand.
The supply of energy is far more than the actual demand.
The energy crisis is the concern that the world's demands on limited natural resources are diminishing as the demand rises.
The non-renewable energy resources will last long.
The non-renewable energy resources can be regained.
Renewable energy has huge potential to provide a solution to the increase in energy crisis.
Renewable energy is the key factor to the future of energy, food and economic security.
We use different forms of energy in our daily lives.
The non-renewable resource of energy is infinite.
Coal is a non-renewable resource.
Natural gas is a non-renewable resource.
Crude oil is a non-renewable resource.
Nuclear energy is a renewable resource.
Non-renewable energy resources occur in limited quantities.
Non-renewable energy resources occur in exhaustible quantities.
Non-renewable energy resources can be regenerated in a short time.
Renewable energy resources can be regenerated in a short time.
Non-renewable energy resources cannot be used again and again.
Non-renewable energy resources can be used again and again.
Renewable energy resources can be used again and again.
Renewable energy sources are also called non-conventional energy sources.
Non-renewable energy sources are also called non-conventional energy sources.
Natural processes continuously replenish renewable energy sources.
Non-renewable energy is generated by wind, solar, hydro, tides, etc.
The energy generated by using wind power is renewable.
The energy generated by using solar power is renewable.
The energy generated by using hydropower is non-renewable.
The energy generated by using tides is renewable.
Renewable energy cannot be exhausted easily.
Renewable energy can be generated at a constant rate for their use over and over again.
Renewable energy cannot be produced or generated through natural processes.
Renewable energy can be produced at a rate greater than or equal to the rate of their consumption.
Non-renewable energy sources are available only in limited supply.
Non-renewable resources are replenished naturally.
Renewable resources are replenished over short periods.
Renewable resources are in a virtually unlimited supply.
When fossil fuel is burnt, the chemical energy is converted into other energy.
Coal is formed in a way similar to the other fossil fuels.
Coal is a uniform substance.
The composition of coal varies from deposit to deposit.
Natural gas is also a major source of energy in our country.
Oil and gas fields always contain some gases.
The formation of natural gas is as long as oil.
Natural gas can be found in independent deposits within the ground.
Methane is a common gas found in swamps.
Methane is a by-product of animals' digestive systems.
Nuclear energy is renewable.
Uranium fuels are replenishable.
Uranium is used in fusion reactions.
Nitrogen is the main constituent of fossil fuels.
Solar is a non-renewable energy source.
Wind is a renewable energy source.
Hydropower is a non-renewable energy source.
Ocean is a renewable energy source.
Geothermal is a renewable energy source.
Biomass is a non-renewable energy source.
Humans have harnessed solar energy since ancient times.
The sunlight that reaches the ground consists of visible light.
The sunlight that reaches the ground consists of infrared.
The sunlight that reaches the ground consists of ultraviolet.
The sunlight that reaches the ground consists of electromagnetic radiation.
Insolation is a commonly used term for the irradiance of sunlight.
A solar cell is a semiconductor diode.
The perfect perpendicular sun rays are the optimal orientation for the solar panel.
Solar heating can play an important role in increasing the impact of global warming.
Harnessing the wind is highly dependent on weather and location.
Wind energy is one of the oldest and cleanest forms of energy.
Wind energy is the most developed of the non-renewable energy sources.
The amount of power that can be harvested from wind depends on the size of the turbine.
The amount of power that can be harvested from wind depends on the length of the blades.
The wind power is proportional to the dimensions of the rotor.
The flowing water or water stored in huge dams is a very important source of energy.
Hydropower is also known as hydroelectric power.
Hydroelectric power is one of the newest and largest sources of renewable energy.
Hydroelectric power is an affordable source of electricity that costs less than most other sources of energy.
The impoundment facility is a small-scale hydropower system.
In the impoundment facility, water is collected and stored at a higher elevation.
The useful power output of hydropower is inversely proportional to the density of water.
The useful power output of hydropower is proportional to the pressure head of the dam.
The useful power output of hydropower is proportional to the volume flow rate of water.
Turbines anchored to the ocean floor could be used to generate power.
Ammonia is a volatile liquid.
The energy potential from the sea is quite large.
The efficient commercial exploitation of energy from the sea is easy.
The tidal energy is harnessed by constructing a dam across a wide opening to the sea.
The water is hotter at the sea surface than at the deeper section.
Geothermal energy is one of the newest types of natural sources of heat.
Thermal energy from magma can be used to generate electricity.
Wells up to a mile deep or more are drilled into underground reservoirs to tap into the geothermal resources.
Animal manure is a form of biomass.
Human sewage is not a form of biomass.
Direct combustion or burning of biomass produces heat.
Thermochemical conversion of biomass produces solid, gaseous, and liquid fuels.
Chemical conversion of biomass produces liquid fuels.
The biological conversion of biomass produces liquid and gaseous fuels.
All biomass can be burned directly for heating.
Pyrolysis and gasification are not thermal decomposition processes.
Transesterification is a kind of chemical conversion process.
Fermentation is a kind of biological conversion of biomass.
Ethanol is used as a vehicle fuel.
Biogas or biomethane is not a natural gas.
Biogas or biomethane is produced in anaerobic digesters at sewage treatment plants and dairy and livestock operations.
Properly treated renewable natural gas has the same uses as fossil fuel natural gas.
